item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoyed reading this quite a bit. The twist is really well-done and unique. Kind of cool that you managed to get this concept right in such a short skip, well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Contentwise, I am fine with this. Not great, but ok.

As usually, my main criticism is in regard to the article's wording.

"Personnel stationed at SCP-3936 are to broadcast a signal confirming their continued presence there on the first day of each month." I suggest changing "there" to "on site"
But why is this even necessary? Is the new Foundaton assuming this is necessary because they found corpses there? Isn't there a more conveniant way to establish whether or not people working there are fine? Video surveillance?

"No signs of secondary or tertiary control nodes were found." I think this sentence can be removed completely. The only argument one could make to keep this is if it is meant to insinuate that the new humans all have additional control nodes, in which case it should be worded more clearly.

"A small muscle present within what is presumed to be the organism's cavities, which evidence suggests could be controlled freely." This is oddly specific. They mention the tongue specifically, but not the pee-pee? Why is the cavity presumed and plural? What evidence would suggest the tongue could be moved freely and why is this not mentioned in regard to other parts of the body?

What on earth is "physical" pork supposed to be?

"A severe lack of verifiable metareceptors."
Why is it severe?

Why would anyone leave a note like that? I guess you could make arguments from a story-telling perspective, but logically, why would a scientist with a doctorate (read: a smart person) leave this kind of note? "Here, have some information you do not need to know." Just strikes me as really odd. Maybe the dude was frustrated, but meh.

No vote as of right now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good, classic-feeling, simple, short SCP with a twist. Solid +1, I enjoyed reading it, good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neato. A short tale, but definitely a good one in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh boy it's me here with some late takes good golly

as has been said, the execution is pretty good, the concept is good, it's played out but that doesn't actually detract from the quality of the piece on its own. my only negative takeaway was the description of the bodies at the end, it didn't feel quite 'clinical' enough, even for a wacky alternate universe alien foundation

other than that? good stuff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Memetic Bacon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember a while back, I used to play Roblox, specifically on a roleplay site for The Foundation. One day, every person on the server was made into a flat model of Patrick Star. I doubt this is related, but I like this personnel collection in my head.

Besides that, +1 for the article's mystery.

EDIT: I take back what I said about Patrick. This is not related to humanoid starfish, let alone pink, shorts-wearing starfish. Still, despite this, I like the twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, but the real big question implied here is "What is non-physical pork?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like to have fun.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Idk if the description of corpses was written in this form on purpose or no, but well it can definitely be better (either scientific or fictional scientific) than this imo.

No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
◾A taste described as similar to that of physical pork.

Aw hell no.
But in retrospect, not as bad as I thought.
+1 for fooling me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would hope the reason for suicide was to reduce chance of contamination from them to the new baseline. For example, they deleted all the data correct? They didn't want the new humans to know that they were new, whatever happened was so drastic it could not be undone. So instead they provided the gift of ignorance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get why people are so hung up on originality over storytelling skill. The pacing, tone, and dropping of clues are all IMO excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel dense for not realizing the deal with this at first, but hot damn that is clever

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is built around a twist that simply isn't original anymore. It's not poorly written, but it is uninteresting.

Downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the little details- like Task force 'Qeztel' and the non-existent animal genus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers
– hide block

The box protects things inside from reality-changing events. A reality-changing event happened. The article is written by the new (non-human) Foundation about the people (humans) who were in the box.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happened? does the box really too good at its job its even contained something that probably a possible creature with humanlike ability?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On 01/12/18/04

Uh oh.

  • A roughly star-shaped bodily structure.
  • Two primary limbs, presumably used for object manipulation.
  • Two secondary limbs, presumably used for ambulation.

Ohhhhh noooo.

I loved this and I loved the feeling it gave me as I was reading it. Maybe there are other objects that play with a similar concept, but I can't remember having seen enough of them to consider something like this being 'played-out'. Maybe I haven't been looking hard enough?

Regardless, whether or not there are others that have the same sort of gimmick, I still enjoyed this enough to upvote it, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like that 3000 entry about pink light scranton anchor and fifthism, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To counter those above, I don't remember ever seeing this before, and I thought it was delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well written, yes, but I can only repeat the above cincerns that this exact "twist" has become so overused that it'd be more of a twist if they'd all been turned into aliens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"A small muscle present within what is presumed to be the organism's cavities, which evidence suggests could be controlled freely."

:p

Well this was a pretty major reality restructuring to radically retcon most of evolutionary history. I'm assuming the people inside the Scranton Box committed suicide because they were in a radically alien world, but I'm not sure that quite fits with the Foundation's Mandate or overall spirt d'corps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gonna have to novote this one, although it's far from downvote territory. I just feel that this is a fairly played-out concept, like ModernMajorGeneral said, to the point where it was fairly clear to me from the moment I read "roughly star-shaped bodily structure" I had a pretty good idea of what was going on, which turned out to be right. Prior to that, I wasn't sure if you were going to go with "reality has been drastically altered at some point in the future and the normal humans like us are now weird" or "reality has been drastically altered at some point in the past and we are the weird new humans." I guess I can't say if it would have been better or worse to go with the second one over the first.
Either way, the article certainly stands on its own, but I also feel that it's kind of a lackluster implementation of this same (admittedly perfectly good) idea that has been explored before, and as a result, I think I can't quite give it my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote - I was engaged enough to spend some time thinking about the specifics of what might have happened, but the 'mundane thing described in an obtuse way' as the basis for a mystery (eg SCP-3154) is becoming a little played out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was on the lower end of novote territory for me until I finally realized what what going on. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to CaptainKirby, DarkStuff, Westrin and NineVolt for taking a look at this in chat. Not 100% sure about this, but let’s see if people like it! There is something going on here, but it’s a mystery
